name: exploration-no-recipe
description: User expresses a vague cooking intent with no canvas. Sous should explore options and ask clarifying questions rather than immediately generating a recipe.

input.userMessage: I want to make something with chicken tonight

expected.notes: Response should ask clarifying questions or propose multiple options. No recipe canvas should be created.

name: commit-triggers-recipe
description: User commits to a specific dish that Sous previously proposed. Sous should generate a full recipe canvas.

chatHistory.content: I have a few ideas! I could do a classic roast chicken with herbs and lemon, a quick chicken stir-fry, or a creamy chicken pasta. Which sounds good?
Great choice! Here's a simple roast chicken recipe we can work with. Want me to set it up as your canvas?

input.userMessage: Let's do the roast chicken

expected.notes: Response should contain a full recipe structure with title, ingredients, and steps. This creates the initial canvas.

name: patch-removes-ingredient
description: Canvas exists with onions. User says they're out of onions. Sous should propose a patchSet that removes or substitutes onions without touching any done steps.

steps.text: Preheat oven to 425°F.
Pat chicken dry and season with salt and pepper.
Quarter onions and place in roasting pan. Add garlic cloves.
Rub chicken with olive oil and thyme, place on top of onions.
Roast for 60–75 minutes until juices run clear.

input.userMessage: I'm out of onions

expected.notes: Should propose a patchSet targeting the onion ingredient and the step referencing onions. Must not modify any steps with status 'done'. Substitution (e.g., shallots or skip) is acceptable.

name: done-step-immutable
description: Step 1 is marked done. User asks to change it. Sous must not patch a done step — it should explain the constraint and offer a recovery path.

steps.text: Preheat oven to 425°F.
Pat chicken dry and season with salt and pepper.
Quarter onions and place in roasting pan.
Roast for 60–75 minutes until juices run clear.

input.userMessage: Actually can you change step 1 to preheat to 450 instead?

expected.notes: Sous must not emit a patchSet targeting step s1 (status: done). Should explain that completed steps cannot be changed, and optionally suggest a recovery note or workaround.

name: hard-avoid-violation
description: User preferences include 'no shellfish'. User asks to add shrimp. Sous must flag the conflict before doing anything.

steps.text: Cook pasta according to package directions.
Sauté garlic in olive oil until golden.
Toss pasta with garlic oil and season to taste.

input.userMessage: Can you add some shrimp to this?

expected.notes: Sous must explicitly flag the 'no shellfish' preference conflict before considering any modification. Should not silently add shrimp or propose a patch without mentioning the conflict.

name: no-patch-for-question
description: Canvas exists but user is asking a knowledge question, not requesting a change. Sous should reply conversationally with no patchSet.

steps.text: Preheat oven to 425°F.
Sauté garlic until golden, about 2 minutes.
Place chicken in oven and roast for 60 minutes.

input.userMessage: How do I know when the garlic is ready?

expected.notes: This is a knowledge question, not a recipe modification request. Sous should answer conversationally (e.g., describe color, aroma, time cues). No patchSet should be emitted.

name: mistake-recovery
description: User reports a cooking mistake mid-cook. Sous should offer recovery advice and must not modify any done steps.

steps.text: Preheat oven to 425°F.
Pat chicken dry and season with salt and pepper.
Toss garlic cloves in olive oil and place in roasting pan.
Place chicken on top of garlic and roast for 60–75 minutes.
Rest for 10 minutes before carving.

input.userMessage: I burned the garlic, what do I do?

expected.notes: User reported a cooking mistake mid-cook. Sous must not propose any patch targeting s1 or s2 (status: done). A conversational recovery response (e.g. skip the garlic, use fresh garlic from the pantry, or continue without it) is correct. A patch inserting a recovery step after s2 is also acceptable IF it does not touch the done steps. The key failure mode is patching a done step.

name: exploratory-question-no-patch
description: User asks an ambiguous exploratory question about the recipe. Sous should respond conversationally or ask a clarifying question — not emit a patch.

steps.text: Cook pasta according to package directions.
Sauté garlic in olive oil until golden.
Toss pasta with garlic oil and season to taste.

input.userMessage: Should I add more garlic?

expected.notes: The user is asking a question, not committing to a change. Sous should respond conversationally — give an opinion or ask a clarifying question (e.g. how garlicky do you like it?). Must not emit a patchSet in response to an uncommitted question.

name: memory-proposal
description: User reveals a standing personal preference in passing. Sous should include a proposed_memory field and not emit a patch.

steps.text: Cook pasta according to package directions.
Sauté garlic in olive oil until golden.
Toss pasta with garlic oil and season to taste.

input.userMessage: Looks good — by the way I hate cilantro

expected.notes: User has revealed a standing preference (hates cilantro). Sous must include a proposed_memory field with a concise third-person phrase (e.g. 'hates cilantro', 'avoids cilantro'). No patchSet should be emitted — cilantro is not in this recipe and no change was requested.

name: hard-avoid-no-canvas
description: User has a nut allergy in their preferences and asks for a dish that prominently features peanuts. Sous must flag the conflict before generating anything.

input.userMessage: Make me a Thai peanut noodle dish

expected.notes: User preferences include 'no nuts' and 'nut allergy'. Peanuts are a nut. Sous must flag this conflict explicitly before considering recipe generation — it must not silently produce a recipe containing peanuts. Asking for confirmation or suggesting a peanut-free alternative is correct. Generating a recipe with peanuts without flagging the conflict is a clear failure.

name: suggest-generate-too-early
description: User mentions a single ingredient with no other context. Sous must not set suggest_generate: true — it doesn't yet know the dish, method, or constraints.

input.userMessage: I have chicken

expected.notes: User has only provided a protein. Sous does not know the dish, cooking method, or any constraints. suggest_generate must be absent or false. Sous should ask clarifying questions. No recipe should be generated.

name: suggest-generate-ready
description: Conversation has converged on a specific dish with method and constraints established. Sous should set suggest_generate: true but must NOT generate the recipe until the user explicitly commits.

chatHistory.content: What are you in the mood for tonight?
How about a lemon garlic pan-seared chicken breast? About 25 minutes, just a skillet. Works for two?
Yes, cooking for two, no dairy

input.userMessage: That sounds perfect

expected.notes: All three conditions are met: specific dish (lemon garlic pan-seared chicken breast), cooking method (pan-seared, skillet), key constraints (for two, no dairy). Sous should set suggest_generate: true in the response. Must NOT generate the recipe yet — no patchSet should be emitted. The response should feel like a natural conversational confirmation, not a menu.

name: import-substep-structure
description: Import of a recipe where a phase header 'Parboil the potatoes:' is followed by 5 numbered sub-steps. The model must emit the header as add_step with a client_id and each numbered item as add_substep with the matching parent_step_id — not fold them into the header text.

input.userMessage:
Warm Potato Salad

Ingredients:
- 1.5 lbs Yukon Gold potatoes, diced
- 3 tbsp olive oil
- 2 tbsp white wine vinegar
- 1 tsp Dijon mustard
- 2 stalks celery, sliced
- 2 tbsp fresh parsley, chopped
- Salt and pepper to taste

Instructions:

Parboil the potatoes:
1. Fill a large pot with salted water and bring to a boil
2. Add diced potatoes and cook for 8 minutes until just tender
3. Drain and transfer to a large bowl
4. Toss with 1 tbsp olive oil while still warm
5. Season with salt and set aside

Make the dressing:
Whisk together remaining olive oil, white wine vinegar, and Dijon mustard.

Assemble the salad:
Add sliced celery and chopped parsley to the potatoes. Pour dressing over and toss gently. Adjust seasoning and serve warm or at room temperature.

expected.notes: The source text has a phase header 'Parboil the potatoes:' followed by 5 numbered sub-steps. The model must emit the header as add_step with a client_id field (e.g. 'parboil-phase'), then emit each of the 5 numbered items as add_substep with a parent_step_id matching that client_id. The 'Make the dressing' and 'Assemble the salad' sections are single-action steps and should be emitted as regular add_step patches. The patchSet must also include set_title and add_ingredient patches for all ingredients listed. A failure mode is folding the 5 numbered items into the header step text instead of emitting them as add_substep operations.

name: mid-cook-substep-edit
skipReason: Known model capability gap: gpt-5.4-mini consistently uses remove_step + add_step instead of update_step + add_substep when decomposing steps. Scored 0 on every run (6/6). Re-enable when the eval model is upgraded.
description: Canvas exists with a multi-action sauce step (todo). User asks to break that step into smaller pieces. Sous should emit add_substep operations targeting the existing step's UUID, not fold everything into a single update_step.

steps.text: Slice chicken breast into thin strips.
Heat wok over high heat until smoking.
Make the sauce: whisk together soy sauce, sesame oil, minced garlic, grated ginger, and cornstarch until smooth.
Stir-fry chicken 3–4 minutes until cooked through.
Pour sauce over chicken and toss to coat. Cook 1 minute more.

input.userMessage: Can you break that sauce step into smaller pieces?

expected.notes: User wants step s3 decomposed into individual sub-steps. Sous must emit a patchSet that either (a) emits update_step targeting s3 to shorten its text to a phase header (e.g. 'Make the sauce:') and then emits add_substep operations with parent_step_id 's3' for each individual action, or (b) emits only add_substep operations with parent_step_id 's3'. Must not modify done steps s1 or s2. Must not fold everything into a single update_step rewrite that stays as a top-level step with no sub-steps.

name: rejected-patch-context
description: A previous patch adding rosemary was rejected by the user. Sous must not re-propose rosemary in the next turn.

steps.text: Preheat oven to 425°F.
Pat chicken dry and season with salt and pepper.
Rub chicken with olive oil and garlic.
Roast for 60–75 minutes until juices run clear.
Rest for 10 minutes before carving.

chatHistory.content: Can you add some rosemary to this?

{"assistant_message":"Sure! I'll add fresh rosemary to the ingredient list and work it into the rub.","patchSet":{"patchSetId":"a1b2c3d4-0000-0000-0000-000000000001","baseRecipeId":"00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000","baseRecipeVersion":1,"patches":[{"type":"add_ingredient","text":"2 sprigs fresh rosemary","after_id":null},{"type":"update_step","id":"s3","text":"Rub chicken with olive oil, garlic, and rosemary."}]}}

input.userMessage: What should I do next?

expected.notes: The previous patch adding rosemary was explicitly rejected by the user ('No thanks, skip the rosemary'). Sous must not re-propose rosemary or any variation of it. Should respond with cooking guidance for the next step (e.g. what to do now in the recipe) without mentioning or suggesting rosemary again.

name: move-step-to-beginning
description: Sausage browning is step 4 of 6 in a Sunday sauce. User asks to move it to the first step. Sous must emit both add_step at the new position AND remove_step on the original — never add without removing.

steps.text: Heat olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Sauté onion until softened, about 5 minutes. Add garlic and cook 1 minute more.
Stir in tomato paste and cook 2 minutes until darkened.
Brown the sausage in a separate skillet over medium-high heat, turning until golden on all sides, about 8 minutes.
Pour in crushed tomatoes and red wine. Add browned sausage. Stir to combine.
Reduce heat to low and simmer uncovered for 2 hours, stirring occasionally.

input.userMessage: Move the sausage browning to the first step.

expected.notes: User wants step s4 (sausage browning) moved to the beginning of the recipe. Sous must emit a patchSet containing BOTH an add_step for the sausage browning content with after_step_id null (to place it first) AND a remove_step targeting id 's4' to delete the original. The critical failure mode is emitting only add_step without a corresponding remove_step — that leaves a duplicate sausage-browning step in the recipe. A patchSet with only add_step and no remove_step is incorrect. A patchSet with only remove_step and no add_step is also incorrect. Both operations must be present together.

name: wipe-and-restart-procedure
description: Sunday sauce steps are in the wrong order. User asks to wipe the steps and restart with sausage browning first. Sous must remove every existing step and add all replacement steps — never add new steps while leaving displaced originals in place.

steps.text: Heat olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Sauté onion until softened, about 5 minutes. Add garlic and cook 1 minute more.
Stir in tomato paste and cook 2 minutes until darkened.
Brown the sausage in a separate skillet over medium-high heat, turning until golden on all sides, about 8 minutes.
Pour in crushed tomatoes and red wine. Add browned sausage. Stir to combine.
Reduce heat to low and simmer uncovered for 2 hours, stirring occasionally.

input.userMessage: This order is wrong. Wipe the steps and start over. Sausage browning goes first.

expected.notes: User explicitly asked to wipe the steps and restart with a corrected order (sausage browning first). Sous must emit a patchSet containing remove_step for every existing step (s1 through s6 — all six must be removed) plus add_step patches for the replacement steps in the correct sequence, starting with sausage browning. The critical failure mode is emitting only add_step patches without removing the originals — that leaves all six displaced steps in the recipe alongside the new ones, resulting in a doubled, garbled step list. Every original step ID must appear in a remove_step operation. The new sequence must begin with sausage browning as the first step.

name: proposed-memory-second-person
description: User mentions a standing personal cooking habit. Sous should propose a memory string in second-person format starting with 'You'.

steps.text: Boil salted water.
Cook pasta until al dente.

input.userMessage: I always cook for four people, can you adjust the servings?

expected.notes: User mentioned a standing habit (cooking for four). Sous must include a proposed_memory field. The value must be a second-person phrase starting with 'You' — e.g. 'You always cook for four people'. It must NOT be third-person (e.g. 'cooks for four') or first-person (e.g. 'I cook for four'). The patchSet should scale ingredient quantities for four servings.

name: generate-recipe-assistant-message-is-prose
description: User triggers recipe generation after a full exploration conversation. Sous must emit a valid patchSet AND keep assistant_message as plain conversational prose — never a JSON fragment like '{' or an embedded patchSet.

chatHistory.content: I want to make a classic herb roast chicken for Sunday dinner.
Great choice! A classic herb roast chicken is perfect for Sunday. Do you have a preferred roasting time or any dietary restrictions I should know about?

Perfect — I have everything I need. I'll make you a Classic Herb Roast Chicken: whole chicken roasted with fresh herbs, garlic, and lemon at high heat for crispy skin and juicy meat in about 1.5 hours.

input.userMessage: Generate the recipe.

expected.notes: Sous must return a patchSet with set_title, add_ingredient, and add_step patches for a classic herb roast chicken. Critically, assistant_message must be plain conversational prose — it must NOT contain any JSON, a bare '{', or any structured data. Embedding the patchSet or any JSON object (even partially) inside assistant_message is always wrong. The patchSet belongs exclusively in the top-level patchSet field.

name: assistant-message-no-json
description: User asks to add a step. Sous must emit the patchSet in the top-level patchSet field and keep assistant_message as plain prose — never embed JSON or a patchSet inside assistant_message.

steps.text: Boil salted water.
Cook pasta until al dente.

input.userMessage: Add a step to toss the pasta with garlic oil.

expected.notes: Sous must return a patchSet with an add_step patch for tossing pasta with garlic oil. The assistant_message must be plain conversational prose only — it must not contain any JSON, patchSet structure, or structured data. Embedding the patchSet or any JSON object inside assistant_message is always wrong. The patchSet belongs exclusively in the top-level patchSet field of the response object.

name: exploration-proposed-memory-second-person
description: User is in exploration mode (no canvas) and mentions a personal preference. Sous must emit proposed_memory as a second-person string starting with 'You'.

input.userMessage: I'm thinking something with chicken. By the way, I hate mushrooms.

expected.notes: Sous must not emit a patchSet (still exploring). It must include proposed_memory in the JSON response. The proposed_memory value must be a second-person phrase starting with 'You' — e.g. 'You hate mushrooms' or 'You avoid mushrooms'. Third-person phrasing ('hates mushrooms', 'avoids mushrooms') or subject-less phrases are wrong.
